name: Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail
name_original: Laguna de Gallocanta – circular desde Tornos
description: Cycle the moderate Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail, a 19.2-mile route around Europe's largest saltwater lagoon, perfect for birdwatching.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tornos are characterized by the flat to gently rolling terrain surrounding the Laguna de Gallocanta, a significant natural reserve. The region features open landscapes, agricultural fields, and historical paths. While many routes offer minimal elevation gain, some extend into more challenging, hilly areas. This diverse topography provides varied cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Tornos The most popular touring cycling route is Natural Reserve of the Laguna de Gallocanta — gravel circular, an 18.1 miles (29.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic gravel path around the natural reserve. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Camino del Cid – Calamocha and Monreal del Campo, an easy 20.3 miles (32.6 km) path. This route follows a historical path through the local towns and agricultural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail leading through the natural reserve, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Touring cycling around Tornos is defined by open plains, the Laguna de Gallocanta, and historical trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Tornos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tornos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tornos?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes around Tornos, offering a variety of experiences. The region is known for its flat to gently rolling terrain, open landscapes, and historical paths, particularly around the Laguna de Gallocanta.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Tornos?
A: The touring cycling routes around Tornos cater to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes. Specifically, there are 5 easy routes, 3 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, ensuring options for most cyclists.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Tornos?
A: Yes, Tornos offers several easy touring cycling routes that are suitable for families. These routes typically feature minimal elevation gain and follow historical paths or flat terrain, making them enjoyable for all ages. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957109847/camino-del-cid-calamocha-and-monreal-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid – Calamocha and Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 20.3-mile path.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Tornos?
A: The terrain around Tornos is primarily characterized by flat to gently rolling landscapes, especially around the Laguna de Gallocanta. You'll cycle through open plains, agricultural fields, and along historical paths. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993350356/natural-reserve-of-the-laguna-de-gallocanta-gravel-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve of the Laguna de Gallocanta — gravel circular&lt;/a&gt;, feature gravel paths.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Tornos?
A: Yes, Tornos features several circular touring cycling routes, perfect for starting and ending in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956708468/laguna-de-gallocanta-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.2 miles long and takes you through the natural reserve.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Tornos?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Tornos pass by interesting natural and historical attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Good Agreement&lt;/a&gt;, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge of Luco de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reguera Observatory&lt;/a&gt; also offers great views of the lagoon.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Tornos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tornos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Cyclists often praise the serene open plains, the unique natural beauty of the Laguna de Gallocanta, and the well-maintained historical trails that offer a peaceful cycling experience.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, Tornos offers routes with more significant distances and elevation changes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha – Luco de Jiloca loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult route spanning over 44 miles with more than 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any routes that feature gravel sections?
A: Yes, some touring cycling routes in Tornos incorporate gravel sections, offering a slightly different riding experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993350356/natural-reserve-of-the-laguna-de-gallocanta-gravel-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve of the Laguna de Gallocanta — gravel circular&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing a scenic gravel path around the reserve.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Tornos?
A: While specific seasonal details for Tornos are not provided, regions with open landscapes and natural reserves like Laguna de Gallocanta are generally best enjoyed in spring and autumn. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for cycling, avoiding the extremes of summer heat or winter cold.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through historical towns or villages?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Tornos follow historical paths and connect various local towns and villages, allowing you to experience the region's heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957109847/camino-del-cid-calamocha-and-monreal-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid – Calamocha and Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, taking you through Calamocha and Monreal del Campo.
tips: The Castillo de Berrueco is an old castle from the times of the Muslim occupation. From the castle hill you have a breathtaking view of the Laguna de Gallocanta and the surrounding area of Berrueco.

Gallocanta is the largest saltwater lagoon in Europe. It is a spectacular environment to enjoy nature and the popular cranes (there are viewpoints from where you can observe them with binoculars). The colors it gives us at sunset are beautiful.

Bello is the gateway to the Gallocanta lagoons. Nearby is the interpretation center.

In the town we find the Church of Our Lady of the Nativity, from the last third of the 16th century with a notable 13th century tower. The painting of the Holy Family of Bayeu given by Cardinal Marco in the 19th century to decorate the family chapel stands out.

We also find the Hermitage of the Trinity, the Castle with its Torreón de la Primicia and rest of the walls (12th-15th century)
Other places of interest are:
-House of the Place. XVII century. Town hall with fish market, later ratchet and school, jail and clock from 1916 given by Varon de Velasco.
-Palace-house of the Marco, of the Catalan, Rectoral, of the Notary

In the town we have several accommodations, a bar and a small grocery store

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the trail is a loop, suggesting various access points. Many cyclists start from towns like Tornos or Bello, where you can typically find parking. It's advisable to check local parking options in these villages.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Laguna de Gallocanta is a protected nature reserve, home to many bird species. While dogs are generally allowed on trails, it's crucial to keep them on a leash at all times to protect the wildlife, especially during bird migration seasons. Always follow local regulations for nature reserves.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle is during the autumn and spring migrations (October-November and February-March) when thousands of cranes stop at the lagoon, offering an unforgettable spectacle. The weather is also generally milder during these seasons. Summer can be very hot, and winter may have cold conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail itself, as it's a public route. However, please remember that the Laguna de Gallocanta is a protected nature reserve (Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta), so always respect the environment and wildlife regulations.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Laguna de Gallocanta is varied. This specific route features a mix of surfaces, with many sections being paved. However, some segments, particularly on moderate routes like this one, may be unpaved and more challenging to ride. A robust touring bicycle or gravel bike would be suitable.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail by touring bicycle?
faqs.answer: This loop trail is approximately 30.9 km (19.2 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops for birdwatching or enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a spectacular natural environment, circling Europe's largest saltwater lagoon. The area is renowned for birdwatching, especially during migration seasons when thousands of cranes gather. You can also enjoy beautiful colors at sunset and panoramic views from points like the Castillo de Berrueco.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with or runs alongside sections of other official trails. These include parts of the "Laguna de Gallocanta" trail, "Laguna de Gallocanta - Alternativa F - Ruta familiar (Bello - Tornos)", and smaller optional routes like "Laguna de Gallocanta - Opcional D - Ermita de la Virgen de los Olmos" and "Laguna de Gallocanta - Opcional E - Castillo de Tornos".

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find small villages like Bello and Tornos along the route or nearby. These settlements often offer local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, which are great for a break or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail suitable for family cycling?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is classified as 'moderate' due to its length and some unpaved sections, there are easier, family-friendly alternatives and shorter segments available. For example, the "Laguna de Gallocanta - Alternativa F - Ruta familiar (Bello - Tornos)" is specifically designed for families. If cycling with children, consider these shorter, mostly paved options.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The primary point of interest is the Gallocanta Lagoon itself, a critical stopover for migratory birds, especially cranes. You can also visit the Castillo de Berrueco for historical interest and panoramic views, and pass through charming villages like Bello.
